completed_tasks.name: Rust stat aggregation commands, TS wrappers, query hooks, test mocks
StatsTab UI with stat cards, ranked lists, seasonal calendar, per-species stats
PDF and CSV export with Comlink worker and export actions
Design token and component governance baseline
Critical-path test coverage for startup and insights flow
Seasonal insight models and stats UI blocks

decisions.decision: Startup DB error dialog now invokes backend quit_app directly
decisions.rationale: Window close on macOS can leave the app process alive; backend quit exits reliably

decisions.decision: Seasonal guidance was added after core stats/export work instead of expanding Phase 4 directly
decisions.rationale: Keeps reporting features stable while layering actionable insights as follow-on work

decisions.decision: Lazy-loaded tabs are the default performance strategy for heavy surfaces
decisions.rationale: Improves startup and tab-switching performance without changing user workflows

decisions.decision: Updater releases now support both tag-push and release-published GitHub flows
decisions.rationale: GitHub Desktop/tag behavior is inconsistent enough that release-published needs to be a supported fallback trigger

decisions.decision: The updater public key is stored directly in tauri.conf.json
decisions.rationale: The public key is safe to publish and bundling must not depend on a runtime override or placeholder secret value

next_action: Verify the next published updater build on a real installed client, then continue maintenance on remaining map regressions (satellite/reporting mismatch and any zone-edit edge cases) or pick the new updater UX backlog item for manual/background checks.
context_notes: As of 2026-05-07, release automation, in-app updater wiring, visible version/update UI, and map/folder edit fixes have landed. Current operational risk is no longer feature plumbing but production verification: make sure GitHub Releases produce correct updater artifacts and that installed clients actually surface and apply updates as expected.
